howItWorks.heading: Platform Features
howItWorks.subheading: Everything you need to understand your body, track your health, and connect with others.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Track cycles, explore body changes, and learn how your body works.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Our system analyzes your logged data to spot potential irregularities and generates PDF health reports you can easily share.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Join the secure Friends Hub for peer support, and explore expert-reviewed articles and videos about your body.

cycleTracker.predictedPeriod: Predicted period: based on your last 3 cycles

riskAnalysis.title: Risk Analysis Overview
riskAnalysis.notEnoughData: Not enough data to determine risk level.
riskAnalysis.logMore: Log at least 3 to unlock your analysis.

risk.lowMsg: Your cycle data looks healthy. Keep tracking to maintain accurate insights.
risk.moderateMsg: Some irregularities detected in your cycle data. Review the factors below.
risk.highMsg: Based on your recent cycles, irregularities in intensity and duration were detected. Please review the factors below or consult a specialist.
risk.unknownMsg: Not enough data to determine risk level.

education.subtitle: No taboos, just the real talk you need about your health and body. Choose a section below to get started.

videos.v1Desc: A quick 5-min guide to the 4 phases of your menstrual cycle.
videos.v2Desc: What to eat during different phases to feel your best.
videos.v3Desc: Doctors react to common misconceptions about periods.

m1.myth: You should not exercise during your period
m1.fact: Light exercise can actually help reduce cramps and improve mood

m2.myth: You cannot take a bath during menstruation
m2.fact: Maintaining hygiene, including bathing, is important and safe

m3.fact: It is a natural biological process involving shedding of the uterine lining

m4.myth: PMS is just in your head
m4.fact: PMS is a real medical condition caused by hormonal changes during the cycle

m5.fact: Mild discomfort is common, but severe pain may need medical attention

m6.myth: You can't get pregnant while on your period
m6.fact: While less likely, sperm can survive for days, making pregnancy possible during your period

quizzes.title: Test your knowledge.
quizzes.subtitle: Take our interactive quizzes to see how much you know about menstrual health.
